description: Unified IT Asset Management (ITAM) and vulnerability risk assessment across ServiceNow (ALM, CMDB, Vulnerability Response) and external scanners, rolled up to business services and asset owners.

raw_schemas.table_ddl:
-- ServiceNow ITAM Asset table (alm_asset)
CREATE TABLE sn_alm_asset (
  sys_id STRING NOT NULL,
  asset_tag STRING,
  display_name STRING,
  serial_number STRING,
  model STRING,
  model_category STRING,
  asset_type STRING,
  subcategory STRING,
  status STRING,
  install_status STRING,
  owned_by STRING,
  used_by STRING,
  department STRING,
  cost_center STRING,
  location STRING,
  company STRING,
  env STRING,
  ci STRING,
  u_business_service STRING,
  acquisition_date TIMESTAMP,
  warranty_expiration TIMESTAMP,
  lease_end TIMESTAMP,
  purchase_price NUMERIC,
  depreciation NUMERIC,
  sys_created_on TIMESTAMP,
  sys_updated_on TIMESTAMP
);

-- ServiceNow CMDB CI base table (cmdb_ci)
CREATE TABLE sn_cmdb_ci (
  sys_id STRING NOT NULL,
  name STRING,
  sys_class_name STRING,
  fqdn STRING,
  ip_address STRING,
  os STRING,
  os_version STRING,
  environment STRING,
  install_status STRING,
  u_business_service STRING,
  owned_by STRING,
  support_group STRING,
  location STRING,
  company STRING,
  hardware_status STRING,
  sys_created_on TIMESTAMP,
  sys_updated_on TIMESTAMP
);

-- ServiceNow Vulnerability definition (sn_vul_vulnerability)
CREATE TABLE sn_vul_vulnerability (
  sys_id STRING NOT NULL,
  number STRING,
  name STRING,
  cve_id STRING,
  cvss_score NUMERIC,
  cvss_vector STRING,
  severity STRING,
  published_date TIMESTAMP,
  remediation_target_date TIMESTAMP,
  remediation_owner STRING,
  source STRING,
  sys_created_on TIMESTAMP,
  sys_updated_on TIMESTAMP
);

-- ServiceNow Vulnerable Item (sn_vul_vulnerable_item)
CREATE TABLE sn_vul_vulnerable_item (
  sys_id STRING NOT NULL,
  vulnerability STRING,
  cmdb_ci STRING,
  state STRING,
  risk_score NUMERIC,
  severity STRING,
  opened TIMESTAMP,
  due_date TIMESTAMP,
  closed TIMESTAMP,
  remediation_status STRING,
  exception_status STRING,
  source STRING,
  sys_created_on TIMESTAMP,
  sys_updated_on TIMESTAMP
);

-- External vulnerability scanner findings (e.g., Qualys/Tenable/etc.)
CREATE TABLE ext_vuln_findings (
  finding_id STRING NOT NULL,
  scanner_source STRING,
  scan_id STRING,
  asset_hostname STRING,
  asset_ip STRING,
  asset_tag STRING,
  cve_id STRING,
  title STRING,
  severity STRING,
  cvss_score NUMERIC,
  first_seen TIMESTAMP,
  last_seen TIMESTAMP,
  last_fixed_attempt TIMESTAMP,
  status STRING,
  remediation_due_date TIMESTAMP,
  patch_available BOOLEAN,
  exploitation_in_the_wild BOOLEAN
);

-- External asset inventory from scanner (optional, to crosswalk with CMDB/ALM)
CREATE TABLE ext_asset_inventory (
  asset_id STRING NOT NULL,
  scanner_source STRING,
  hostname STRING,
  ip_address STRING,
  asset_tag STRING,
  os STRING,
  os_version STRING,
  site_name STRING,
  business_unit STRING,
  env STRING,
  criticality STRING,
  first_seen TIMESTAMP,
  last_seen TIMESTAMP
);

-- Unified business service dimension across ITAM/CMDB
CREATE TABLE business_services (
  service_id STRING NOT NULL,
  source_system STRING,
  source_service_key STRING,
  service_name STRING,
  service_tier STRING,
  business_unit STRING,
  env STRING,
  criticality INTEGER,
  owner_group STRING,
  created_at TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP
);

lod_configurations.description: One row per ServiceNow ITAM asset
One row per CMDB CI and vulnerability pairing
One row per external scanner finding
One row per external scanner asset

user_query: Build an ITAM and vulnerability risk dashboard that shows asset coverage, vulnerable asset counts, exposure windows, and risk scores by business service, asset owner, environment, and criticality. Correlate ServiceNow ALM/CMDB assets and vulnerable items with external scanner findings (by hostname/IP/asset tag) to give a unified risk posture.
data_mart_goals: Provide a unified IT asset inventory across ServiceNow ALM, CMDB, and external scanner assets.
Quantify vulnerability exposure per asset, CI, and business service, including counts of open critical/high vulns and average exposure window.
Highlight coverage gaps where assets exist in ITAM/CMDB but are not scanned, or scanner assets have no CMDB/ALM match.
Compute composite risk scores per service and per owner, combining asset criticality, vulnerability severity, and exposure duration.
metric_questions: How many in-scope assets do we have by asset_type, env, and business_unit?
What percentage of assets have at least one open critical or high vulnerability?
What is the average and p90 exposure window (days open) for critical vulnerabilities by business service?
Which asset owners or support groups have the highest backlog of vulnerable items?
Which services have the highest risk scores when combining ITAM criticality and vulnerability severity?
Where are the biggest coverage gaps (assets in ALM/CMDB not seen by scanner, and scanner assets not mapped to CMDB/ALM)?
What is the trend of open vs closed vulnerable items and external findings over the last 12 months?
What is the remediation performance (time-to-fix) by scanner_source, owner_group, and business_unit?

cleaning_steps: Ensure sys_id and asset_tag are unique per asset where possible.
Normalize asset_type, model_category, and status values to a controlled vocabulary.
Standardize env and location values (e.g., PROD/DEV/UAT).
Derive business_unit from department, cost_center, or company where missing.
Ensure sys_id is unique per CI.
Normalize environment, os, and os_version values.
Flag CIs with missing u_business_service or owned_by for follow-up.
Standardize name/fqdn to enable reliable hostname joins with external scanner assets.
Ensure cve_id format is valid where present.
Bucket severity into standardized levels (e.g., Critical/High/Medium/Low).
Handle missing or null cvss_score values with defaults or flags.
Derive age_since_published in days for risk modelling.
Ensure vulnerability and cmdb_ci foreign keys exist in their source tables.
Normalize state, remediation_status, and exception_status values.
Compute days_open as difference between closed and opened (or now if still open).
Flag items with due_date in the past and not closed as overdue.
Normalize severity and status across different scanner_sources.
Ensure cve_id, asset_hostname, and asset_ip formats are valid.
Compute exposure_days as difference between last_seen and first_seen.
Derive a unified severity_score from cvss_score and exploitation_in_the_wild.
Normalize hostname (case, domain suffix) to improve join quality.
Standardize env and business_unit values.
Derive criticality buckets (e.g., Tier 0/1/2) from criticality text.
Flag assets with first_seen very recent and no CMDB/ALM mapping as potential shadow IT.
